title: oreobatus neomexicanus
intro: Oreobatus neomexicanus, also known as the New Mexican penstemon or Penstemon neomexicanus, is a flowering plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Oreobatus neomexicanus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
